ai_insights.executive_summary: PCI's February 2026 performance reflects strong underlying discipline with meaningful month-over-month gains across scheduling compliance and patient flow, though asset utilization remains unevenly distributed across the five-clinic footprint. Scheduler Compliance improved sharply at four clinics—Fayatteville-SI gained 23.6 points from 54.5% in January to 78.1% in February, Pcinewnan gained 16.4 points from 52.5% to 68.9%, and Pci-Atlanta gained 18.9 points from 61.3% to 80.2%—bringing these sites closer to the company average of 77.84%. However, the portfolio's Performance Score distribution (ranging from 38 to 75 out of 100, where 50 is network average) signals pockets of operational friction that warrant targeted attention.
highlights: PciStockbridge delivered the strongest absolute performance with a score of 75 out of 100, anchored by Scheduler Compliance of 87.2% (9.4 points above company average) and a dramatic 74.2% reduction in minutes past close per patient, falling from 22.2 minutes in January to 5.8 minutes in February. PciSandySprings and PciLocustGrove both demonstrated exceptional patient flow discipline: SandySprings reduced average patient delay from 13.7 minutes in January to 10.2 minutes in February (a 25.9% improvement), while LocustGrove achieved the portfolio's lowest delay at 4.9 minutes against the company average of 13.18 minutes, positioning both as benchmarks for scheduling execution.
concerns: We noticed Pci Fayatteville is running 22.4 minutes of average patient delay compared to the company average of 13.18 minutes, a 70% gap that represents the largest outlier in the network (1.8 standard deviations above the mean). Concurrent with this, Pci-Atlanta is finishing 2.2 treatments past closing daily against a portfolio average of 0.7, suggesting flow breakdowns in the late-shift window. We also observed that Pci-Atlanta's nurse utilization has declined over two consecutive months (3-month average: 60.5%) despite 100% iAssign adoption, which may indicate a mismatch between assignment protocols and actual staffing capacity that warrants a closer look at the nurse-to-chair assignment audit trail.
recommendations: We recommend that Pci Fayatteville conduct a structured review of its scheduling templates and chair-assignment sequencing to address the 22.4-minute average patient delay—the single largest performance gap in the network. A concrete starting point would be to audit the clinic's current iOptimize recommendation adherence (currently 74.2%) against actual scheduled slot patterns for the next two weeks, comparing how many recommendations were overridden and why, so we can identify whether the gap stems from template misalignment, patient no-show absorption, or chair capacity constraints. This targeted exercise should surface the root cause and unlock faster movement toward the company average.

ai_insights.executive_summary: PCI delivered solid operational momentum in March 2026, with five clinics showing consistent three-month improvements in scheduler compliance and seven clinics reducing treatments running past closing time. The network's composite performance reflects mixed outcomes: while LocustGrove (69/100), Stockbridge (72/100), and Fayatteville-SI (83/100) all exceeded the network average of 50/100, three clinics remain at or near that midpoint, anchored primarily by scheduling and patient flow challenges rather than systemic breakdowns.
highlights: Pci Fayatteville demonstrated the most dramatic operational turnaround in closing-time management, reducing treatments past closing from 0.7 daily in February 2026 to 0.2 in March 2026, an 66.2% improvement. Fayatteville-SI continues to lead the network with a performance score of 83/100 (33 points above the network average of 50/100) and delivered a 27.7% reduction in average patient delay from 10.4 minutes in February 2026 to 7.5 minutes in March 2026, well below the company benchmark of 12.65 minutes. Scheduler compliance is advancing across the board: Pci Fayatteville's compliance climbed from 73.5% three-month average to 87.3% this month, and Fayatteville-SI's compliance rose to 90.9%, both showing sustained momentum over three consecutive months with R² values above 0.96.
concerns: Pci Fayatteville's average patient delay of 22.4 minutes represents a significant outlier, sitting 77% above the company average of 12.65 minutes and 1.8 standard deviations above the group mean—we noticed this gap persists despite strong scheduler compliance improvements, suggesting the constraint may lie in chair-to-nurse assignment fluidity or treatment duration variability rather than scheduling discipline alone. Pci-Atlanta's closure-time pressure remains elevated at 1.5 treatments running past closing daily (248.8% above the group mean of 0.5), and while this clinic shows three-month improvement in scheduler compliance (now 89.6%, up from a 77.0% three-month average), the operational bottleneck has not yet coincided with corresponding relief in daily overtime; we should explore whether current iAssign adoption rates or nurse utilization levels are constraining throughput. Additionally, Pci LocustGrove's chair utilization sits at 33.6%, representing a 58.2% shortfall below the group mean of 76.9%—this under-deployment deserves investigation into patient volume patterns, scheduling template assumptions, or potential capacity misalignment relative to regional demand.
recommendations: We recommend that Pci Fayatteville pilot a structured audit of nurse-to-chair assignments and treatment sequencing workflows, beginning with a one-week shadow review of morning and afternoon chair rotations to identify whether average delay stems from pre-treatment prep time, assessment bottlenecks, or downstream scheduling gaps between consecutive patients. Given the clinic's strong scheduler compliance at 87.3% alongside the outsized 22.4-minute delay versus the 12.65-minute company benchmark, the root cause most likely resides in operational execution rather than appointment adherence—this targeted review will clarify whether iAssign adoption acceleration or nurse-shift realignment could unlock the efficiency gain needed to bring delay in line with network peers.

scheduler_compliance.label: Scheduler Compliance
scheduler_compliance.explanation: Percentage of appointments scheduled following iOptimize recommendations. Higher is better. Company average is typically 60-70%. OncoSmart benchmark target is 75%.

avg_delay_mins.label: Avg Delay
avg_delay_mins.explanation: Average daily schedule delay in minutes. Lower is better. Measures how far behind the clinic is running on average.

avg_treatments_per_day.label: Tx Past Close/Day
avg_treatments_per_day.explanation: Average treatments running past treatment close per day (overtime patients per day). Lower is better.

avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.label: Tx Mins Past Close/Patient
avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.explanation: Average treatment minutes past closing time per patient. Lower is better.

avg_chair_utilization.label: Chair Utilization
avg_chair_utilization.explanation: Average chair utilization rate. Higher is better.

iassign_utilization.label: iAssign Utilization
iassign_utilization.explanation: Percentage of nurse assignments completed via iAssign. Higher means more consistent, optimized assignments.

avg_patients_per_nurse.label: Patients/Nurse/Day
avg_patients_per_nurse.explanation: Average patients per nurse per day. Context-dependent -- too high means understaffing, too low means inefficiency.

avg_chairs_per_nurse.label: Chairs/Nurse
avg_chairs_per_nurse.explanation: Average chairs assigned per nurse. Context-dependent.

avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.label: Nurse Utilization
avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.explanation: Average nurse-to-patient in-chair time per day. Higher is better.

chatbot_context.data_notes: Company Avg rows represent the network average across all locations for this client in the given month. Onco rows are global oncology benchmarks from the OncoSmart network. Rows named Global Avg, Network Avg, Total, or Overall are network-wide aggregates, not individual clinic locations. MoM (Month-over-Month) deltas compare the current month to the prior month. Composite scores are weighted 0 to 100 where 100 is best-in-class performance.
